lines: Find your position for sleep. [long pause] Adjust your pillow. [long pause] Shift your hips until the mattress meets you fully. [long pause] Pull the blanket up if you need it. [long pause] Tuck it around your shoulders if that feels comforting. [long pause] There's no rush. [long pause] Take whatever time you need to settle.

Notice where your body touches the bed. [long pause] The back of your head, cradled in the pillow. [long pause] Your shoulder blades pressing gently against the mattress. [long pause] Your lower back, finding its curve. [long pause] The backs of your arms. [long pause] Your calves. [long pause] Your heels. [long pause] These are the places where gravity does the work for you. [long pause] You don't have to hold yourself up anymore. [long pause] The bed is holding you. [long pause] It has been waiting all day to hold you.

Let your hands rest wherever they've fallen. [long pause] Palms up, palms down — it doesn't matter. [long pause] Let your jaw hang open slightly. [long pause] Let your tongue drop from the roof of your mouth. [long pause] Let the small muscles around your eyes go slack. [long pause] Even the muscles in your ears can relax — let them. [long pause] Let your whole face become still.

Take one deep breath in through your nose... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] filling your chest and belly... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and let it fall out through your mouth with a sigh.

Again. [long pause] In through the nose, slowly... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and out with a long sigh. [long pause] Let the exhale carry some of the day with it. [long pause] Whatever you were holding — let it ride out on the breath.
One more. [long pause] Deep breath in... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] and out. [long pause] Let everything go. [long pause] Good.

Now let your breathing go. [long pause] You don't need to control it anymore. [long pause] Just let it move at whatever pace it wants. [long pause] You're a passenger now. [long pause] Just watching the breath come and go, like watching waves from a distance.

Tonight we practice autogenic training — an old, evidence-based method for teaching your body to relax itself. [long pause] It was developed nearly a hundred years ago, and it works by giving the body simple suggestions: heaviness, warmth, calm. [long pause] You won't have to do much. [long pause] Just repeat some simple phrases in your mind and notice what happens. [long pause] The body knows how to let go. [long pause] We're just giving it permission.

We'll pair this with words from Isaiah, chapter forty-three. [long pause] The prophet speaks God's voice to a people who are afraid — a people in exile, far from home, uncertain of everything. [long pause] And into that fear, God says: "Fear not, for I have redeemed you. [long pause] I have called you by name. [long pause] You are mine. [long pause] When you pass through the waters, I will be with you."

Called by name. [long pause] Not by your title at work. [long pause] Not by your role in anyone else's life. [long pause] Not by what you accomplished or failed to accomplish today. [long pause] By your name. [long pause] The name that was spoken before you had a resume, before you had responsibilities, before you had anything to prove. [long pause] You are known. [long pause] You are claimed.

Now bring your attention to your right arm. [long pause] Don't move it. [long pause] Just notice it. [long pause] Feel its weight against the bed. [long pause] Feel the fabric of the sheet against your skin. [long pause] Notice the temperature — is it warm? [long pause] [long pause] Cool? [long pause] [long pause] Just notice.
Silently repeat with me: My right arm is heavy.
Feel the heaviness settling in. [long pause] Like your arm is sinking into the mattress, leaving an impression. [long pause] Like it's becoming part of the bed.

Now feel the warmth in that arm. [long pause] Maybe it's subtle — just the faintest glow of heat. [long pause] Maybe it's the warmth of blood moving through your veins, the warmth of a body that has been working all day and is finally at rest.
My right arm is heavy and warm. [long pause] Pleasantly heavy. [long pause] Pleasantly warm. [long pause] It sinks deeper into the bed, like a stone settling into sand.
Now shift your attention to your left arm. [long pause] Same thing. [long pause] No effort. [long pause] Just noticing. [long pause] Feel its weight, its shape, the place where it meets the mattress.

Both arms now. [long pause] Heavy and warm. [long pause] Like they've been resting in a patch of late afternoon sunlight. [long pause] Like smooth stones on a warm riverbank, absorbing heat all day, now radiating it gently into the evening air.

Move your attention down to your legs. [long pause] Start with your right leg. [long pause] Feel its full length — hip to thigh, thigh to knee, knee to calf, calf to ankle, ankle to the sole of your foot. [long pause] The whole long, heavy weight of it.
The warmth spreads through your right leg like sunlight moving slowly across the surface of a lake. [long pause] From hip to toes. [long pause] Slow and golden.

Both legs heavy and warm. [long pause] They don't need to carry you anywhere tonight. [long pause] They've walked you through another day, and now their work is done. [long pause] Every step they took today — to the kitchen, to the car, up the stairs, down the hall — all of it complete. [long pause] Let them rest completely.

Now notice your heartbeat. [long pause] You might feel it in your chest. [long pause] You might feel it in your throat, or in the tips of your fingers, or deep in your belly. [long pause] Just find it. [long pause] That quiet, steady pulse. [long pause] It doesn't need you to notice it — but notice it anyway. [long pause] Give it a moment of your attention, the way you'd notice a friend who's been quietly working beside you all day.
My heartbeat is calm and regular.
My heartbeat is calm and regular.
My heartbeat is calm and regular.

It has been beating since before you were born. [long pause] A hundred thousand times today. [long pause] It doesn't need your supervision. [long pause] It doesn't need your worry. [long pause] It knows its rhythm the way a river knows its course. [long pause] Trust it.

Now your breathing. [long pause] Don't change it. [long pause] Don't deepen it or slow it down. [long pause] Just watch it move. [long pause] In and out. [long pause] In and out. [long pause] Like a door swinging gently on its hinges.
The breath moves on its own.
The breath moves on its own.
The breath moves on its own.

You're not breathing. [long pause] You're being breathed. [long pause] Something deeper than your will, older than your consciousness, keeps this going — breath after breath, all through the night. [long pause] Even in the deepest sleep, when you are unaware of everything, you are sustained. [long pause] Held. [long pause] Kept alive by a faithfulness you didn't earn and can't exhaust.

Bring your awareness to your abdomen. [long pause] The soft center of your body. [long pause] Feel its gentle rise and fall. [long pause] The way it rounds on the inhale and settles on the exhale. [long pause] So simple. [long pause] So reliable.

The warmth radiates outward from your center like a gentle fire. [long pause] Not burning — just glowing. [long pause] A warmth that spreads through your whole torso, through your back, into your hips. [long pause] A deep, internal comfort. [long pause] The kind of warmth you feel when you come inside from the cold and sit down and realize... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] you're safe. [long pause] You're home.

Finally, your forehead. [long pause] The place where tension lives. [long pause] Where the day's worries carve their lines. [long pause] Where concentration furrows the brow and anxiety tightens the skin.
My forehead is cool and smooth.
My forehead is cool and smooth.
My forehead is cool and smooth.

The day's furrowed brow relaxes. [long pause] The muscles around your eyes soften. [long pause] Your temples ease. [long pause] The skin of your forehead becomes wide and open. [long pause] Your whole face becomes still, like the surface of calm water on a windless night.

Your entire body is now in a state of gentle release. [long pause] Arms heavy and warm. [long pause] Legs heavy and warm. [long pause] Heartbeat calm and regular. [long pause] Breath moving on its own. [long pause] Abdomen warm. [long pause] Forehead cool and smooth. [long pause] This is your body at rest. [long pause] This is what it feels like when you stop trying to manage everything and simply... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] let it be.
"When you pass through the waters, I will be with you."

Now let an image come to mind. [long pause] You're standing at the edge of still waters. [long pause] A wide, quiet lake at dusk. [long pause] The surface is glass — perfect glass — reflecting the last colors of the sky. [long pause] Amber and rose along the horizon, fading upward through lavender to deep, deep blue. [long pause] Not a ripple. [long pause] Not a breath of wind. [long pause] Just stillness so complete it seems sacred.

If water imagery doesn't feel safe or comfortable to you tonight, you can simply imagine yourself resting beside the lake on a warm bank of soft grass. [long pause] You don't have to go in. [long pause] You can stay at the shore, feel the cool air drifting off the water, listen to the silence, and be at rest. [long pause] The shore is safe. [long pause] You are held here too.

The air is cool and carries the faint scent of cedar and wild sage. [long pause] The trees along the far shore stand perfectly still, their dark reflections unbroken in the water — so perfect you can't tell where the trees end and the reflections begin. [long pause] A bird calls once, far away, and then the silence returns. [long pause] Deeper than before.

If it feels right, you step into the water. [long pause] It's warm — warmer than the air around you. [long pause] Surprisingly warm. [long pause] It rises slowly around your ankles, and you feel the soft, sandy bottom beneath your feet. [long pause] Fine sand, smooth as flour, giving gently under your weight. [long pause] Each step is steady. [long pause] Each step is held.

The water rises to your knees. [long pause] You feel its warmth wrapping around your calves, your shins. [long pause] Then to your waist. [long pause] It doesn't pull at you. [long pause] There's no current, no undertow. [long pause] It simply... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] holds. [long pause] The way a parent's arms hold a child.

You lean back. [long pause] The water rises around your shoulders, your neck, the back of your head. [long pause] And then — without effort — you're floating. [long pause] Your body is supported completely. [long pause] Your arms drift outward. [long pause] Your legs extend. [long pause] Every part of you is held by the water, every muscle free of its burden. [long pause] You weigh nothing here.
"Fear not, for I have redeemed you. [long pause] I have called you by name."

You are known here. [long pause] Not as someone who needs to perform. [long pause] Not as someone who needs to prove their worth. [long pause] Known as you are — the full, complicated, beautiful, imperfect truth of you. [long pause] And still called. [long pause] Still claimed. [long pause] Still wanted.

The sky above is wide and filled with stars. [long pause] So many they seem crowded — thick clusters and scattered trails of light flung across the darkness. [long pause] The Milky Way arches overhead like a river of light. [long pause] Each star placed. [long pause] Each one named. [long pause] Like you.

The water holds you like hands beneath your back. [long pause] You feel your heartbeat, slow and steady, pulsing through the warm water. [long pause] The rhythm of your breath sends tiny ripples outward across the surface, and they disappear into the stillness. [long pause] You and the water and the sky are all one rhythm now.
"When you pass through the waters, I will be with you. [long pause] When you pass through the rivers, they will not sweep over you."

Whatever waters you passed through today — the stress, the weight of obligation, the moments where you felt in over your head — they did not sweep over you. [long pause] You are here. [long pause] You made it through another day. [long pause] And now you are held in waters that do not threaten, do not rush, do not demand anything from you.

God, I bring you this body — tired and heavy with the day. [long pause] I bring you this mind — still turning over things I can't resolve tonight. [long pause] I don't ask you to fix everything. [long pause] I ask you to hold me while I rest. [long pause] The way this water holds me now. [long pause] Effortlessly. [long pause] Completely. [long pause] Without condition. [long pause] I trust you with my sleep. [long pause] I trust you with tomorrow. [long pause] Tonight, I float. [long pause] Amen.

The stars are so bright above you. [long pause] Pick one. [long pause] The brightest one you can see. [long pause] Watch it. [long pause] Let it be the only thing in your field of vision. [long pause] A single point of light in the wide, dark sky.

Your breathing is slow now. [long pause] So slow. [long pause] Your body is heavy and warm. [long pause] The water is warm. [long pause] The night is warm. [long pause] Everything is warm and dark and still.

You don't need to swim. [long pause] You don't need to reach the shore. [long pause] You don't need to do anything at all. [long pause] There is nowhere to go. [long pause] Nothing to solve. [long pause] No one waiting for anything from you.
The One who named you... [long pause] [long pause] [long pause] holds it all.
